methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $42M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Atomic AI is a Series A biotech company that has raised $42M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector at Series A and Series B stages, including peers such as Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $42M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $84M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $805M, with a range spanning $100M (peer floor) to $3.3B (peer ceiling). Public comps anchoring cannot be applied because target revenue is not on file, precluding a P/S multiple application despite a public Biotech & Health median P/S of 6.6x across three tickers.

The two applicable methods produce a wide implied range of $84M to $805M. The triangulated median of $444M reflects material dispersion, driven primarily by the substantial valuation spread within the peer set itself. Key uncertainties include stage mismatch (most peers are Series B, one stage ahead) and heterogeneity in peer business models and capital efficiency. A narrower range would require clarity on target unit economics, IP differentiation, and runway profile relative to peers.
